summary: English Pre-Raphaelite painter. Known for Work (laborers with Thomas Carlyle observing, took 13 years), The Last of England (migrants under umbrella before Dover), and Manchester Murals. Grandson Ford Madox Ford wrote The Good Soldier.

works.name: Work (1852–1865)
works.description: Laborers replacing a roadway; Thomas Carlyle and Frederick Maurice observe. Took 13 years.

clues.clue: Laborers replacing a roadway; Thomas Carlyle and Frederick Maurice observe; took 13 years
examples: Thomas Carlyle can be seen on the right of a canvas that this man spent thirteen years working on

works.name: The Last of England (1855)
works.description: Circular seascape of migrants under umbrella before White Cliffs of Dover.

clues.clue: Migrants huddle under umbrella at sea before White Cliffs of Dover; inspired by Woolner's emigration
examples: a migrant couple huddle under an umbrella at sea before the White Cliffs of Dover

clues.clue: English Pre-Raphaelite; Manchester Murals for Manchester Town Hall
Grandson Ford Madox Ford wrote The Good Soldier
examples: whose grandson wrote The Good Soldier

comprehensive_summary: Ford Madox Brown was an English Pre-Raphaelite painter. Work depicts laborers with Carlyle observing (took 13 years). The Last of England shows migrants under an umbrella before the White Cliffs of Dover. He painted the Manchester Murals. His grandson Ford Madox Ford wrote The Good Soldier.

cards.front: Painting: laborers replacing a roadway observed by Thomas Carlyle; took 13 years
Painting: circular seascape of migrants under umbrella before White Cliffs of Dover
cards.back: The Last of England (Ford Madox Brown)
